channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Mapping Phase Diagrams of Quantum Spin Systems Through Semidefinite-Programming Relaxations
item.description:
Author(s): David Jansen, Donato Farina, Luke Mortimer, Timothy Heightman, Andreas Leitherer, Pere Mujal, Jie Wang, and Antonio Acín

Identifying quantum phase transitions poses a significant challenge in condensed matter physics, as this requires methods that both provide accurate results and scale well with system size. In this work, we demonstrate how relaxation methods can be used to generate the phase diagram for one- and two…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 050401] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): David Jansen, Donato Farina, Luke Mortimer, Timothy Heightman, Andreas Leitherer, Pere Mujal, Jie Wang, and Antonio Acín

Identifying quantum phase transitions poses a significant challenge in condensed matter physics, as this requires methods that both provide accurate results and scale well with system size. In this work, we demonstrate how relaxation methods can be used to generate the phase diagram for one- and two…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 050401] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Mapping Phase Diagrams of Quantum Spin Systems Through Semidefinite-Programming Relaxations
item.dc:creator: David Jansen, Donato Farina, Luke Mortimer, Timothy Heightman, Andreas Leitherer, Pere Mujal, Jie Wang, and Antonio Acín
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Integrable Spin Chains in Twisted Maximally Supersymmetric Yang-Mills Theory
item.description:
Author(s): Tim Meier and Stijn J. van Tongeren

We study an angular dipole deformation of maximally supersymmetric Yang-Mills (SYM) theory that preserves its classical scale invariance. Two-point functions of suitable single trace operators, restricted to an invariant plane, are determined by scaling dimensions computable via an integrable spin c…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 051601] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tim Meier and Stijn J. van Tongeren

We study an angular dipole deformation of maximally supersymmetric Yang-Mills (SYM) theory that preserves its classical scale invariance. Two-point functions of suitable single trace operators, restricted to an invariant plane, are determined by scaling dimensions computable via an integrable spin c…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 051601] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Integrable Spin Chains in Twisted Maximally Supersymmetric Yang-Mills Theory
item.dc:creator: Tim Meier and Stijn J. van Tongeren
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Particles and Fields

item.title: Re-emergence of a Polar Instability at High Pressure in ${\mathrm{KNbO}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): Mohamad Baker Shoker, Sitaram Ramakrishnan, Boris Croes, Olivier Cregut, Nicolas Beyer, Kokou D. Dorkenoo, Pierre Rodière, Björn Wehinger, Gaston Garbarino, Mohamed Mezouar, Marine Verseils, Pierre Fertey, Salia Cherifi-Hertel, Pierre Bouvier, and Mael Guennou

Ferroelectric instabilities in perovskites are known to be suppressed by a moderate hydrostatic pressure. The prediction of their re-entrance in a much higher pressure regime is well accepted theoretically, but a conclusive experimental confirmation is still missing. Here, we show its occurrence in …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056101] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mohamad Baker Shoker, Sitaram Ramakrishnan, Boris Croes, Olivier Cregut, Nicolas Beyer, Kokou D. Dorkenoo, Pierre Rodière, Björn Wehinger, Gaston Garbarino, Mohamed Mezouar, Marine Verseils, Pierre Fertey, Salia Cherifi-Hertel, Pierre Bouvier, and Mael Guennou

Ferroelectric instabilities in perovskites are known to be suppressed by a moderate hydrostatic pressure. The prediction of their re-entrance in a much higher pressure regime is well accepted theoretically, but a conclusive experimental confirmation is still missing. Here, we show its occurrence in …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056101] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Re-emergence of a Polar Instability at High Pressure in ${\mathrm{KNbO}}_{3}$
item.dc:creator: Mohamad Baker Shoker, Sitaram Ramakrishnan, Boris Croes, Olivier Cregut, Nicolas Beyer, Kokou D. Dorkenoo, Pierre Rodière, Björn Wehinger, Gaston Garbarino, Mohamed Mezouar, Marine Verseils, Pierre Fertey, Salia Cherifi-Hertel, Pierre Bouvier, and Mael Guennou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Dynamical Superconducting Parity Effect in a Coulomb Pb Island
item.description:
Author(s): Wenhao Zhang, Xin Liao, James Jun He, Hui-Nan Xia, Tao Xie, Naoto Nagaosa, Tianyou Zhai, and Ying-Shuang Fu

Cooper-pair condensation dynamics plays an indispensable role in a new type of superconducting parity effect in a Coulomb blockade system made up of nanosized Pb islands.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056201] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wenhao Zhang, Xin Liao, James Jun He, Hui-Nan Xia, Tao Xie, Naoto Nagaosa, Tianyou Zhai, and Ying-Shuang Fu

Cooper-pair condensation dynamics plays an indispensable role in a new type of superconducting parity effect in a Coulomb blockade system made up of nanosized Pb islands.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056201] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Dynamical Superconducting Parity Effect in a Coulomb Pb Island
item.dc:creator: Wenhao Zhang, Xin Liao, James Jun He, Hui-Nan Xia, Tao Xie, Naoto Nagaosa, Tianyou Zhai, and Ying-Shuang Fu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Infinite Randomness Criticality and Localization of the Floating Phase in Arrays of Rydberg Atoms Trapped with Nonperfect Tweezers
item.description:
Author(s): Jose Soto-Garcia and Natalia Chepiga

Chains of Rydberg atoms have emerged as a powerful platform for exploring low-dimensional quantum physics. This success originates from the precise control of lattice geometries provided by optical tweezers, which allows access to a wide range of synthetic quantum phases. Experiments on one-dimensio…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056502] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jose Soto-Garcia and Natalia Chepiga

Chains of Rydberg atoms have emerged as a powerful platform for exploring low-dimensional quantum physics. This success originates from the precise control of lattice geometries provided by optical tweezers, which allows access to a wide range of synthetic quantum phases. Experiments on one-dimensio…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056502] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Infinite Randomness Criticality and Localization of the Floating Phase in Arrays of Rydberg Atoms Trapped with Nonperfect Tweezers
item.dc:creator: Jose Soto-Garcia and Natalia Chepiga
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Ultrafast Spin Accumulations Drive Magnetization Reversal in Multilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Harjinder Singh, Alberto Anadón, Junta Igarashi, Quentin Remy, Stéphane Mangin, Michel Hehn, Jon Gorchon, and Gregory Malinowski

Engineering and controlling heat and spin transport on the femtosecond timescale in spintronic devices opens up new ways to manipulate magnetization with unprecedented speed. Yet the underlying reversal mechanisms remain poorly understood due to the challenges of probing ultrafast, nonequilibrium sp…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056701] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Harjinder Singh, Alberto Anadón, Junta Igarashi, Quentin Remy, Stéphane Mangin, Michel Hehn, Jon Gorchon, and Gregory Malinowski

Engineering and controlling heat and spin transport on the femtosecond timescale in spintronic devices opens up new ways to manipulate magnetization with unprecedented speed. Yet the underlying reversal mechanisms remain poorly understood due to the challenges of probing ultrafast, nonequilibrium sp…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056701] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Ultrafast Spin Accumulations Drive Magnetization Reversal in Multilayers
item.dc:creator: Harjinder Singh, Alberto Anadón, Junta Igarashi, Quentin Remy, Stéphane Mangin, Michel Hehn, Jon Gorchon, and Gregory Malinowski
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Ideal Glass and Ideal Disk Packing in Two Dimensions
item.description:
Author(s): Viola M. Bolton-Lum, R. Cameron Dennis, Peter K. Morse, and Eric I. Corwin

A computational minimization procedure shows how to make an ideal glass, a disordered system of particles with zero configurational entropy and with the mechanical and thermal properties of a crystal.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 058201] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Viola M. Bolton-Lum, R. Cameron Dennis, Peter K. Morse, and Eric I. Corwin

A computational minimization procedure shows how to make an ideal glass, a disordered system of particles with zero configurational entropy and with the mechanical and thermal properties of a crystal.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 058201] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Ideal Glass and Ideal Disk Packing in Two Dimensions
item.dc:creator: Viola M. Bolton-Lum, R. Cameron Dennis, Peter K. Morse, and Eric I. Corwin
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Polymers, Chemical Physics, Soft Matter, and Biological Physics
item.prism:section: Polymers, Chemical Physics, Soft Matter, and Biological Physics

item.title: Duality Viewpoint of Noninvertible Symmetry-Protected Topological Phases
item.description:
Author(s): Weiguang Cao, Masahito Yamazaki, and Linhao Li

Recent advancements in generalized symmetries have drawn significant attention to gapped phases of matter exhibiting novel symmetries, such as noninvertible symmetries. By leveraging the duality transformations, the classification and construction of gapped phases with noninvertible symmetry can be …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 040402] Published Fri Jan 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Weiguang Cao, Masahito Yamazaki, and Linhao Li

Recent advancements in generalized symmetries have drawn significant attention to gapped phases of matter exhibiting novel symmetries, such as noninvertible symmetries. By leveraging the duality transformations, the classification and construction of gapped phases with noninvertible symmetry can be …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 040402] Published Fri Jan 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Duality Viewpoint of Noninvertible Symmetry-Protected Topological Phases
item.dc:creator: Weiguang Cao, Masahito Yamazaki, and Linhao Li
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Programmable Lattices for Non-Abelian Topological Photonics and Braiding
item.description:
Author(s): Gyunghun Kim, Jensen Li, Xianji Piao, Namkyoo Park, and Sunkyu Yu

Non-Abelian physics, originating from noncommutative sequences of operations, unveils novel topological degrees of freedom. In photonics, significant efforts have been devoted to developing reconfigurable non-Abelian platforms, serving both as classical testbeds for non-Abelian quantum phenomena and…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 043804] Published Fri Jan 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gyunghun Kim, Jensen Li, Xianji Piao, Namkyoo Park, and Sunkyu Yu

Non-Abelian physics, originating from noncommutative sequences of operations, unveils novel topological degrees of freedom. In photonics, significant efforts have been devoted to developing reconfigurable non-Abelian platforms, serving both as classical testbeds for non-Abelian quantum phenomena and…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 043804] Published Fri Jan 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Programmable Lattices for Non-Abelian Topological Photonics and Braiding
item.dc:creator: Gyunghun Kim, Jensen Li, Xianji Piao, Namkyoo Park, and Sunkyu Yu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Dirac Charge in Antiferromagnetic Topological Semimetals
item.description:
Author(s): Kohei Hattori, Hikaru Watanabe, and Ryotaro Arita

Topological node of electronic bands can carry emergent charge degree of freedom such as the Berry curvature monopole of the Weyl semimetals, which results in intriguing transport and optical phenomena. In this Letter, we discuss the existence of the hidden “Dirac charge” and its detection via the p…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046603] Published Fri Jan 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kohei Hattori, Hikaru Watanabe, and Ryotaro Arita

Topological node of electronic bands can carry emergent charge degree of freedom such as the Berry curvature monopole of the Weyl semimetals, which results in intriguing transport and optical phenomena. In this Letter, we discuss the existence of the hidden “Dirac charge” and its detection via the p…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046603] Published Fri Jan 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Dirac Charge in Antiferromagnetic Topological Semimetals
item.dc:creator: Kohei Hattori, Hikaru Watanabe, and Ryotaro Arita
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Hybrid Acousto-Optical Spin Control in Quantum Dots
item.description:
Author(s): Mateusz Kuniej, Paweł Machnikowski, and Michał Gawełczyk

Mechanical degrees of freedom very weakly couple to spins in semiconductors. The inefficient coupling between phonons and single electron spins in semiconductor quantum dots (QDs) hinders their integration into on-chip acoustically coupled quantum hybrid systems. We propose a hybrid acousto-optical …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046904] Published Fri Jan 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mateusz Kuniej, Paweł Machnikowski, and Michał Gawełczyk

Mechanical degrees of freedom very weakly couple to spins in semiconductors. The inefficient coupling between phonons and single electron spins in semiconductor quantum dots (QDs) hinders their integration into on-chip acoustically coupled quantum hybrid systems. We propose a hybrid acousto-optical …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046904] Published Fri Jan 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Hybrid Acousto-Optical Spin Control in Quantum Dots
item.dc:creator: Mateusz Kuniej, Paweł Machnikowski, and Michał Gawełczyk
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Real-Space Switching of Local Moments Driven by Quantum Geometry in Correlated Graphene Heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Niklas Witt, Siheon Ryee, Lennart Klebl, Jennifer Cano, Giorgio Sangiovanni, and Tim O. Wehling

Hybridization-induced topological transition between Mott states leads to flat bands, providing an alternative to twisted bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046505] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Niklas Witt, Siheon Ryee, Lennart Klebl, Jennifer Cano, Giorgio Sangiovanni, and Tim O. Wehling

Hybridization-induced topological transition between Mott states leads to flat bands, providing an alternative to twisted bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046505] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Real-Space Switching of Local Moments Driven by Quantum Geometry in Correlated Graphene Heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Niklas Witt, Siheon Ryee, Lennart Klebl, Jennifer Cano, Giorgio Sangiovanni, and Tim O. Wehling
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Spontaneous Symmetry Breaking of Cavity Vacuum and Emergent Gyrotropic Effects in Embedded Moiré Superlattices
item.description:
Author(s): Zuzhang Lin, Hsun-Chi Chan, Wenqi Yang, Yixin Sha, Cong Xiao, Shuang Zhang, and Wang Yao

All-to-one coupling between electrons in moiré superlattice to a deep-subwavelength cavity uncovers an unprecedented dual spontaneous parity symmetry breaking simultaneously in the cavity vacuum and the electronic ground state.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046903] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zuzhang Lin, Hsun-Chi Chan, Wenqi Yang, Yixin Sha, Cong Xiao, Shuang Zhang, and Wang Yao

All-to-one coupling between electrons in moiré superlattice to a deep-subwavelength cavity uncovers an unprecedented dual spontaneous parity symmetry breaking simultaneously in the cavity vacuum and the electronic ground state.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046903] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Spontaneous Symmetry Breaking of Cavity Vacuum and Emergent Gyrotropic Effects in Embedded Moiré Superlattices
item.dc:creator: Zuzhang Lin, Hsun-Chi Chan, Wenqi Yang, Yixin Sha, Cong Xiao, Shuang Zhang, and Wang Yao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Excitonic Instability Revealed by the Elastocaloric Effect in ${\mathrm{Ta}}_{2}{\mathrm{NiSe}}_{5}$
item.description:
Author(s): Elliott Rosenberg, Joss Ayres-Sims, Andrew Millis, David Cobden, and Jiun-Haw Chu

Elastocaloric effect measurements on Ta2NiSe5 show that its phase transition at ~324 K is driven by a nonacoustic instability, rather than by an acoustic shear mode, strengthening the case that the transition is largely excitonic in nature.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046503] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Elliott Rosenberg, Joss Ayres-Sims, Andrew Millis, David Cobden, and Jiun-Haw Chu

Elastocaloric effect measurements on Ta2NiSe5 show that its phase transition at ~324 K is driven by a nonacoustic instability, rather than by an acoustic shear mode, strengthening the case that the transition is largely excitonic in nature.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046503] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Excitonic Instability Revealed by the Elastocaloric Effect in ${\mathrm{Ta}}_{2}{\mathrm{NiSe}}_{5}$
item.dc:creator: Elliott Rosenberg, Joss Ayres-Sims, Andrew Millis, David Cobden, and Jiun-Haw Chu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Breaking the Intrinsic Absorption Limit for Arbitrarily Thin Conductive Films at Grazing Incidence
item.description:
Author(s): Yuxuan Liu, Ren-Hao Fan, Dong-Xiang Qi, Ruwen Peng, Yun Lai, Mu Wang, and Jie Luo

Light grazing an ultrathin conductive film can be absorbed much more strongly than previously thought.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046902] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuxuan Liu, Ren-Hao Fan, Dong-Xiang Qi, Ruwen Peng, Yun Lai, Mu Wang, and Jie Luo

Light grazing an ultrathin conductive film can be absorbed much more strongly than previously thought.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046902] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Breaking the Intrinsic Absorption Limit for Arbitrarily Thin Conductive Films at Grazing Incidence
item.dc:creator: Yuxuan Liu, Ren-Hao Fan, Dong-Xiang Qi, Ruwen Peng, Yun Lai, Mu Wang, and Jie Luo
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Topological Vortex and Antivortex Transport in a Three-Dimensional Photonic Disclination Metamaterial
item.description:
Author(s): Yingfeng Qi, Siqi Xu, Bei Yan, Zebin Zhu, Yan Meng, Xiaoyuan Jiao, Linyun Yang, Zhenxiao Zhu, Ziyao Wang, and Zhen Gao

Vortex or antivortex modes that carry orbital angular momentum (OAM) have provided a novel degree of freedom for modern optics and practical applications. However, their robust transport has thus far been limited to two-dimensional photonic systems. Here, we report on the first experimental observat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 043803] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yingfeng Qi, Siqi Xu, Bei Yan, Zebin Zhu, Yan Meng, Xiaoyuan Jiao, Linyun Yang, Zhenxiao Zhu, Ziyao Wang, and Zhen Gao

Vortex or antivortex modes that carry orbital angular momentum (OAM) have provided a novel degree of freedom for modern optics and practical applications. However, their robust transport has thus far been limited to two-dimensional photonic systems. Here, we report on the first experimental observat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 043803] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological Vortex and Antivortex Transport in a Three-Dimensional Photonic Disclination Metamaterial
item.dc:creator: Yingfeng Qi, Siqi Xu, Bei Yan, Zebin Zhu, Yan Meng, Xiaoyuan Jiao, Linyun Yang, Zhenxiao Zhu, Ziyao Wang, and Zhen Gao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Quantized Conductance in a CVD-Grown Nanoribbon with Hidden Rashba Effect
item.description:
Author(s): Jianfei Xiao, Yiwen Ma, Congwei Tan, Kui Zhao, Yunteng Shi, Bingbing Tong, Peiling Li, Ziwei Dou, Xiaohui Song, Guangtong Liu, Jie Shen, Zhaozheng Lyu, Li Lu, Hailin Peng, and Fanming Qu

Quantized conductance in quasi-one-dimensional systems not only provides a hallmark of ballistic transport, but also serves as a gateway for exploring quantum phenomena. Recently, a unique hidden Rashba effect, which arises from the compensation of opposite spin polarizations of a Rashba bilayer in …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046302] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jianfei Xiao, Yiwen Ma, Congwei Tan, Kui Zhao, Yunteng Shi, Bingbing Tong, Peiling Li, Ziwei Dou, Xiaohui Song, Guangtong Liu, Jie Shen, Zhaozheng Lyu, Li Lu, Hailin Peng, and Fanming Qu

Quantized conductance in quasi-one-dimensional systems not only provides a hallmark of ballistic transport, but also serves as a gateway for exploring quantum phenomena. Recently, a unique hidden Rashba effect, which arises from the compensation of opposite spin polarizations of a Rashba bilayer in …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046302] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantized Conductance in a CVD-Grown Nanoribbon with Hidden Rashba Effect
item.dc:creator: Jianfei Xiao, Yiwen Ma, Congwei Tan, Kui Zhao, Yunteng Shi, Bingbing Tong, Peiling Li, Ziwei Dou, Xiaohui Song, Guangtong Liu, Jie Shen, Zhaozheng Lyu, Li Lu, Hailin Peng, and Fanming Qu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Thermopower Probe of Fractional Quantum Hall States in Monolayer Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Nishat Sultana, Robert W. Rienstra, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Joseph A. Stroscio, D. E. Feldman, and Fereshte Ghahari

Thermopower is more sensitive than resistivity in detecting certain fractional quantum Hall states in monolayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046502] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nishat Sultana, Robert W. Rienstra, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Joseph A. Stroscio, D. E. Feldman, and Fereshte Ghahari

Thermopower is more sensitive than resistivity in detecting certain fractional quantum Hall states in monolayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046502] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Thermopower Probe of Fractional Quantum Hall States in Monolayer Graphene
item.dc:creator: Nishat Sultana, Robert W. Rienstra, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Joseph A. Stroscio, D. E. Feldman, and Fereshte Ghahari
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Higher-Form Anomalies on Lattices
item.description:
Author(s): Yitao Feng, Ryohei Kobayashi, Yu-An Chen, and Shinsei Ryu

Higher-form symmetry in a tensor product Hilbert space is always emergent: The symmetry generators become genuinely topological only when the Gauss law is energetically enforced at low energies. In this Letter, we present a general method for defining the ’t Hooft anomaly of higher-form symmetries i…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046504] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yitao Feng, Ryohei Kobayashi, Yu-An Chen, and Shinsei Ryu

Higher-form symmetry in a tensor product Hilbert space is always emergent: The symmetry generators become genuinely topological only when the Gauss law is energetically enforced at low energies. In this Letter, we present a general method for defining the ’t Hooft anomaly of higher-form symmetries i…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046504] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Higher-Form Anomalies on Lattices
item.dc:creator: Yitao Feng, Ryohei Kobayashi, Yu-An Chen, and Shinsei Ryu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Wave-Function-Free Approach for Predicting Nonlinear Responses in Weyl Semimetals
item.description:
Author(s): Mohammad Yahyavi, Ilya Belopolski, Yuanjun Jin, Yilin Zhao, Jinyang Ni, Naizhou Wang, Yi-Chun Hung, Zi-Jia Cheng, Tyler A. Cochran, Tay-Rong Chang, Wei-bo Gao, Su-Yang Xu, Jia-Xin Yin, Qiong Ma, Md Shafayat Hossain, Arun Bansil, Naoto Nagaosa, and Guoqing Chang

By sidestepping the intractable calculations of many-body wave functions, density functional theory has revolutionized the prediction of ground states of materials. However, predicting nonlinear responses—critical for next-generation quantum devices—still relies heavily on explicit wave functions, l…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046601] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mohammad Yahyavi, Ilya Belopolski, Yuanjun Jin, Yilin Zhao, Jinyang Ni, Naizhou Wang, Yi-Chun Hung, Zi-Jia Cheng, Tyler A. Cochran, Tay-Rong Chang, Wei-bo Gao, Su-Yang Xu, Jia-Xin Yin, Qiong Ma, Md Shafayat Hossain, Arun Bansil, Naoto Nagaosa, and Guoqing Chang

By sidestepping the intractable calculations of many-body wave functions, density functional theory has revolutionized the prediction of ground states of materials. However, predicting nonlinear responses—critical for next-generation quantum devices—still relies heavily on explicit wave functions, l…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046601] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Wave-Function-Free Approach for Predicting Nonlinear Responses in Weyl Semimetals
item.dc:creator: Mohammad Yahyavi, Ilya Belopolski, Yuanjun Jin, Yilin Zhao, Jinyang Ni, Naizhou Wang, Yi-Chun Hung, Zi-Jia Cheng, Tyler A. Cochran, Tay-Rong Chang, Wei-bo Gao, Su-Yang Xu, Jia-Xin Yin, Qiong Ma, Md Shafayat Hossain, Arun Bansil, Naoto Nagaosa, and Guoqing Chang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Ferroelectrics Drive Topological Magnon Transitions and Valley Transport
item.description:
Author(s): Yingxi Bai, Bo Yuan, Zhiqi Chen, Ying Dai, Baibiao Huang, Xiaotian Wang, and Chengwang Niu

Topological magnons offer unique opportunities for low-dissipation spin transport, but achieving nonvolatile control over their topological states remains a significant challenge. Here, using a Heisenberg-Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya model and symmetry analysis, we propose a ferroelectrically tunable magno…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046602] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yingxi Bai, Bo Yuan, Zhiqi Chen, Ying Dai, Baibiao Huang, Xiaotian Wang, and Chengwang Niu

Topological magnons offer unique opportunities for low-dissipation spin transport, but achieving nonvolatile control over their topological states remains a significant challenge. Here, using a Heisenberg-Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya model and symmetry analysis, we propose a ferroelectrically tunable magno…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046602] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Ferroelectrics Drive Topological Magnon Transitions and Valley Transport
item.dc:creator: Yingxi Bai, Bo Yuan, Zhiqi Chen, Ying Dai, Baibiao Huang, Xiaotian Wang, and Chengwang Niu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Magnon Torques Mediated by Orbital Hybridization at the Light Metal-Antiferromagnetic Insulator Interface
item.description:
Author(s): Yuchen Pu, Guoyi Shi, Hua Bai, Xinhou Chen, Chenhui Zhang, Zhaohui Li, Mehrdad Elyasi, and Hyunsoo Yang

Magnon torques, which can operate without involving moving electrons, could circumvent the Joule heating issue. In conventional magnon torque systems, the spin source layer with strong spin-orbit coupling is utilized to inject magnons, and the efficiency is limited by the inherent spin Hall conducti…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046701] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuchen Pu, Guoyi Shi, Hua Bai, Xinhou Chen, Chenhui Zhang, Zhaohui Li, Mehrdad Elyasi, and Hyunsoo Yang

Magnon torques, which can operate without involving moving electrons, could circumvent the Joule heating issue. In conventional magnon torque systems, the spin source layer with strong spin-orbit coupling is utilized to inject magnons, and the efficiency is limited by the inherent spin Hall conducti…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046701] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnon Torques Mediated by Orbital Hybridization at the Light Metal-Antiferromagnetic Insulator Interface
item.dc:creator: Yuchen Pu, Guoyi Shi, Hua Bai, Xinhou Chen, Chenhui Zhang, Zhaohui Li, Mehrdad Elyasi, and Hyunsoo Yang

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Nondestructive Optomechanical Detection Scheme for Bose-Einstein Condensates
item.description:
Author(s): Cisco Gooding, Cameron R. D. Bunney, Samin Tajik, Sebastian Erne, Steffen Biermann, Jörg Schmiedmayer, Jorma Louko, William G. Unruh, and Silke Weinfurtner

We present a two-tone heterodyne optical readout scheme to extract unequal-time density correlations along an arbitrary stationary interaction path from a pancake-shaped Bose-Einstein condensate, using a modulated laser probe. Analyzing the measurement noise both from imprecision and backaction, we …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 043401] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Cisco Gooding, Cameron R. D. Bunney, Samin Tajik, Sebastian Erne, Steffen Biermann, Jörg Schmiedmayer, Jorma Louko, William G. Unruh, and Silke Weinfurtner

We present a two-tone heterodyne optical readout scheme to extract unequal-time density correlations along an arbitrary stationary interaction path from a pancake-shaped Bose-Einstein condensate, using a modulated laser probe. Analyzing the measurement noise both from imprecision and backaction, we …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 043401] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Nondestructive Optomechanical Detection Scheme for Bose-Einstein Condensates
item.dc:creator: Cisco Gooding, Cameron R. D. Bunney, Samin Tajik, Sebastian Erne, Steffen Biermann, Jörg Schmiedmayer, Jorma Louko, William G. Unruh, and Silke Weinfurtner

item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Universal Bound States with Bose-Fermi Duality in Microwave-Shielded Ultracold Molecules
item.description:
Author(s): Tingting Shi, Haitian Wang, and Xiaoling Cui

We report universal bound states of microwave-shielded ultracold molecules that solely depend on the strengths of long-range dipolar interaction and microwave coupling. Under a highly elliptic microwave field, few-molecule scatterings in three dimensions are shown to be governed by effective one-dim…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 043402] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tingting Shi, Haitian Wang, and Xiaoling Cui

We report universal bound states of microwave-shielded ultracold molecules that solely depend on the strengths of long-range dipolar interaction and microwave coupling. Under a highly elliptic microwave field, few-molecule scatterings in three dimensions are shown to be governed by effective one-dim…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 043402] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Universal Bound States with Bose-Fermi Duality in Microwave-Shielded Ultracold Molecules

item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Destructive Interference Mediated Topological Transitions in Bilayer Metasurfaces
item.description:
Author(s): Bo Wang, Ruhao Pan, Lechen Yang, Xu Ji, Haifang Yang, and Junjie Li

Optical bound states in the continuum (BICs) are polarization singularities with integer topological charges ($ν$) in momentum space, whose far-field radiation vanishes in the surrounding radiative states. Here, we study the dynamic evolution of topological charges in symmetry-protected BICs within …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 043801] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bo Wang, Ruhao Pan, Lechen Yang, Xu Ji, Haifang Yang, and Junjie Li

Optical bound states in the continuum (BICs) are polarization singularities with integer topological charges (ν) in momentum space, whose far-field radiation vanishes in the surrounding radiative states. Here, we study the dynamic evolution of topological charges in symmetry-protected BICs within bi…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 043801] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Destructive Interference Mediated Topological Transitions in Bilayer Metasurfaces

item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Decoupling the Compositional Fluctuation Theory and Polar Nanoregions in Relaxor Ferroelectric Films
item.description:
Author(s): Feng-Hui Gong, Yu-Ting Chen, Kang-Ming Luo, Hua-Long Ge, Tao Wang, Yun-Long Tang, Jia-Qi Liu, Yu-Jia Wang, Yin-Lian Zhu, and Xiu-Liang Ma

The existence of polar nanoregions (PNRs) endows relaxor ferroelectrics with peculiar behaviors, which is explained using the compositional fluctuation theory (CFT). Here, by designing relaxor ferroelectric films with the same configurational entropy (${S}_{\text{config}}$), namely ${\mathrm{ATiO}}_…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046801] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Feng-Hui Gong, Yu-Ting Chen, Kang-Ming Luo, Hua-Long Ge, Tao Wang, Yun-Long Tang, Jia-Qi Liu, Yu-Jia Wang, Yin-Lian Zhu, and Xiu-Liang Ma

The existence of polar nanoregions (PNRs) endows relaxor ferroelectrics with peculiar behaviors, which is explained using the compositional fluctuation theory (CFT). Here, by designing relaxor ferroelectric films with the same configurational entropy (Sconfig), namely ATiO3 and PbBO3, we show that t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046801] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Decoupling the Compositional Fluctuation Theory and Polar Nanoregions in Relaxor Ferroelectric Films
item.dc:creator: Feng-Hui Gong, Yu-Ting Chen, Kang-Ming Luo, Hua-Long Ge, Tao Wang, Yun-Long Tang, Jia-Qi Liu, Yu-Jia Wang, Yin-Lian Zhu, and Xiu-Liang Ma

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum-Metric-Based Optical Selection Rules
item.description:
Author(s): Yongpan Li and Cheng-Cheng Liu

The optical selection rules dictate symmetry-allowed and forbidden transitions, playing a decisive role in engineering exciton quantum states and designing optoelectronic devices. While both the real (quantum metric) and imaginary (Berry curvature) parts of quantum geometry contribute to optical tra…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046901] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yongpan Li and Cheng-Cheng Liu

The optical selection rules dictate symmetry-allowed and forbidden transitions, playing a decisive role in engineering exciton quantum states and designing optoelectronic devices. While both the real (quantum metric) and imaginary (Berry curvature) parts of quantum geometry contribute to optical tra…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046901] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum-Metric-Based Optical Selection Rules
item.dc:creator: Yongpan Li and Cheng-Cheng Liu

item.title: Orbital Ordering in the Charge Density Wave Phases of ${\mathrm{BaNi}}_{2}({\mathrm{As}}_{1\text{−}x}{\mathrm{P}}_{x}{)}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Tom Lacmann, Robert Eder, Igor Vinograd, Michael Merz, Mehdi Frachet, Philippa Helen McGuinness, Kurt Kummer, Enrico Schierle, Amir-Abbas Haghighirad, Sofia-Michaela Souliou, and Matthieu Le Tacon

We use resonant x-ray scattering at the nickel ${\mathrm{L}}_{2,3}$ edges to investigate the interplay between orbital degrees of freedom and charge density waves (CDWs) in the superconductor ${\mathrm{BaNi}}_{2}({\mathrm{As}}_{1\text{−}x}{\mathrm{P}}_{x}{)}_{2}$. Both the incommensurate and commens…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036504] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tom Lacmann, Robert Eder, Igor Vinograd, Michael Merz, Mehdi Frachet, Philippa Helen McGuinness, Kurt Kummer, Enrico Schierle, Amir-Abbas Haghighirad, Sofia-Michaela Souliou, and Matthieu Le Tacon

We use resonant x-ray scattering at the nickel L2,3 edges to investigate the interplay between orbital degrees of freedom and charge density waves (CDWs) in the superconductor BaNi2(As1−xPx)2. Both the incommensurate and commensurate CDWs in this system exhibit strong resonant enhancement with disti…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036504] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Orbital Ordering in the Charge Density Wave Phases of ${\mathrm{BaNi}}_{2}({\mathrm{As}}_{1\text{−}x}{\mathrm{P}}_{x}{)}_{2}$

item.title: Enhanced Anomalous Nernst Effect in the Ferromagnetic Kondo Lattice ${\mathrm{CeCo}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Shuyue Guan, Weian Guo, Pengyu Zheng, Xinxuan Lin, Yuqing Huang, Jiawei Li, Xiao-Bin Qiang, Longfei Li, Weiwei Xie, Hai-Zhou Lu, Zhiping Yin, and Shuang Jia

The anomalous Nernst effect (ANE), generating a voltage perpendicular to a temperature gradient due to magnetization, is closely linked to the Berry curvature (BC) near the Fermi energy in topological magnets. We report an enhanced spontaneous ANE in the ferromagnetic Kondo lattice ${\mathrm{CeCo}}_…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036505] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shuyue Guan, Weian Guo, Pengyu Zheng, Xinxuan Lin, Yuqing Huang, Jiawei Li, Xiao-Bin Qiang, Longfei Li, Weiwei Xie, Hai-Zhou Lu, Zhiping Yin, and Shuang Jia

The anomalous Nernst effect (ANE), generating a voltage perpendicular to a temperature gradient due to magnetization, is closely linked to the Berry curvature (BC) near the Fermi energy in topological magnets. We report an enhanced spontaneous ANE in the ferromagnetic Kondo lattice CeCo2As2, which f…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036505] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Enhanced Anomalous Nernst Effect in the Ferromagnetic Kondo Lattice ${\mathrm{CeCo}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Shuyue Guan, Weian Guo, Pengyu Zheng, Xinxuan Lin, Yuqing Huang, Jiawei Li, Xiao-Bin Qiang, Longfei Li, Weiwei Xie, Hai-Zhou Lu, Zhiping Yin, and Shuang Jia

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantized Transport of $ν=2/3$ Fractional Quantum Hall Edge with Disordered Superconducting Proximity
item.description:
Author(s): Pok Man Tam, Hao Chen, and Biao Lian

Quantum Hall edge states in proximity to a superconductor (SC) usually acquire a nonquantized electron-to-hole conversion probability in transport, due to nonuniversal SC couplings and disorders. With counterpropagating modes, we show that the situation can be the opposite in the $ν=2/3$ fractional …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036602] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pok Man Tam, Hao Chen, and Biao Lian

Quantum Hall edge states in proximity to a superconductor (SC) usually acquire a nonquantized electron-to-hole conversion probability in transport, due to nonuniversal SC couplings and disorders. With counterpropagating modes, we show that the situation can be the opposite in the ν=2/3 fractional qu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036602] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantized Transport of $ν=2/3$ Fractional Quantum Hall Edge with Disordered Superconducting Proximity
item.dc:creator: Pok Man Tam, Hao Chen, and Biao Lian

item.title: Experimental Evidence of Néel-Order-Driven Magneto-optical Kerr Effect in an Altermagnetic Insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Haolin Pan, Rui-Chun Xiao, Jiahao Han, Hongxing Zhu, Junxue Li, Qian Niu, Yang Gao, and Dazhi Hou

The magneto-optical Kerr effect (MOKE) is investigated in hematite, a collinear antiferromagnetic insulator, across a broad wavelength spectrum. By combining the optical measurements with magnetometry results, we unambiguously demonstrate that the Néel-order contribution dominates the MOKE signal, w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036701] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haolin Pan, Rui-Chun Xiao, Jiahao Han, Hongxing Zhu, Junxue Li, Qian Niu, Yang Gao, and Dazhi Hou

The magneto-optical Kerr effect (MOKE) is investigated in hematite, a collinear antiferromagnetic insulator, across a broad wavelength spectrum. By combining the optical measurements with magnetometry results, we unambiguously demonstrate that the Néel-order contribution dominates the MOKE signal, w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036701] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Experimental Evidence of Néel-Order-Driven Magneto-optical Kerr Effect in an Altermagnetic Insulator

item.title: Quasiresonant Regime of Surface Plasmon for Broad Angular Responsivity of Plasmonic Diffraction
item.description:
Author(s): Koya Okazaki, Nobukazu Teranishi, and Atsushi Ono

We propose plasmonic diffraction under quasiresonant regime of surface plasmon (quasi-SPR) that exhibits broad incident-angle responsivity. Numerical simulations reveal that quasi-SPR induces efficient and large-angle diffraction toward the transmission side over a wide range of oblique incidences b…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036902] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Koya Okazaki, Nobukazu Teranishi, and Atsushi Ono

We propose plasmonic diffraction under quasiresonant regime of surface plasmon (quasi-SPR) that exhibits broad incident-angle responsivity. Numerical simulations reveal that quasi-SPR induces efficient and large-angle diffraction toward the transmission side over a wide range of oblique incidences b…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036902] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026

item.description:

A mechanical network of flexible links can be designed to solve a problem in matrix algebra.

item.title: Control of Molecular Rotation in Helium Nanodroplets with an Optical Centrifuge
item.description:
Author(s): Ian MacPhail-Bartley, Alexander A. Milner, Frank Stienkemeier, and Valery Milner

A technique for spinning up molecules in a gas has now been adapted to work with superfluid helium as the host medium.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 033002] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026

item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Spin-Chain Multichannel Kondo Model via Image Impurity Boundary Condition
item.description:
Author(s): Jordan Gaines, Guangjie Li, and Jukka I. Väyrynen

One of the signature observables for the electronic multichannel Kondo model is the impurity entropy, which was found in ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ Heisenberg chains with the open boundary condition (OBC) and periodic boundary condition (PBC), for the one-channel and two-channel cases, respectively. H…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036503] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jordan Gaines, Guangjie Li, and Jukka I. Väyrynen

One of the signature observables for the electronic multichannel Kondo model is the impurity entropy, which was found in J1−J2 Heisenberg chains with the open boundary condition (OBC) and periodic boundary condition (PBC), for the one-channel and two-channel cases, respectively. However, it is not c…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036503] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-Chain Multichannel Kondo Model via Image Impurity Boundary Condition
item.dc:creator: Jordan Gaines, Guangjie Li, and Jukka I. Väyrynen

item.title: Single-Fluid Model for Rotating Annular Supersolids and Its Experimental Implications
item.description:
Author(s): N. Preti, N. Antolini, C. Drevon, P. Lombardi, A. Fioretti, C. Gabbanini, G. Ferioli, G. Modugno, and G. Biagioni

The famous two-fluid model of finite-temperature superfluids has been recently extended to describe the mixed classical-superfluid dynamics of the newly discovered supersolid phase of matter. We show that for rigidly rotating supersolids one can derive a more appropriate single-fluid model, in which…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036001] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): N. Preti, N. Antolini, C. Drevon, P. Lombardi, A. Fioretti, C. Gabbanini, G. Ferioli, G. Modugno, and G. Biagioni

The famous two-fluid model of finite-temperature superfluids has been recently extended to describe the mixed classical-superfluid dynamics of the newly discovered supersolid phase of matter. We show that for rigidly rotating supersolids one can derive a more appropriate single-fluid model, in which…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036001] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Single-Fluid Model for Rotating Annular Supersolids and Its Experimental Implications

item.title: Acoustoelectric Probing of Fractal Energy Spectra in Graphene/hBN Moiré Superlattices
item.description:
Author(s): Wenqing Song, Yicheng Mou, Qing Lan, Guorui Zhao, Zejing Guo, Jiaqi Liu, Tuoyu Zhao, Cheng Zhang, and Wu Shi

Moiré superlattices with long-range periodicity exhibit Hofstadter energy spectra under accessible magnetic fields, enabling the exploration of emergent quantum phenomena through a hierarchy of fractal states. However, higher-order features, located at elevated energies with narrow bandwidths, typic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036301] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wenqing Song, Yicheng Mou, Qing Lan, Guorui Zhao, Zejing Guo, Jiaqi Liu, Tuoyu Zhao, Cheng Zhang, and Wu Shi

Moiré superlattices with long-range periodicity exhibit Hofstadter energy spectra under accessible magnetic fields, enabling the exploration of emergent quantum phenomena through a hierarchy of fractal states. However, higher-order features, located at elevated energies with narrow bandwidths, typic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036301] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Acoustoelectric Probing of Fractal Energy Spectra in Graphene/hBN Moiré Superlattices

item.title: ${\mathrm{FeTa}X}_{2}$: A Ferrimagnetic Quantum Anomalous Hall Insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Yadong Jiang, Huan Wang, and Jing Wang (王靖)

We propose the van der Waals layered ternary transition metal chalcogenides ${\mathrm{FeTa}X}_{2}$ ($X=\mathrm{S}$, Se, Te) as a new family of ferrimagnetic quantum anomalous Hall insulators with a sizable bulk gap and high Chern number $\mathcal{C}=−2$. The magnetic order arises primarily from Fe a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036601] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yadong Jiang, Huan Wang, and Jing Wang (王靖)

We propose the van der Waals layered ternary transition metal chalcogenides FeTaX2 (X=S, Se, Te) as a new family of ferrimagnetic quantum anomalous Hall insulators with a sizable bulk gap and high Chern number C=−2. The magnetic order arises primarily from Fe atoms, whose strong ferromagnetic exchan…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036601] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: ${\mathrm{FeTa}X}_{2}$: A Ferrimagnetic Quantum Anomalous Hall Insulator
item.dc:creator: Yadong Jiang, Huan Wang, and Jing Wang (王靖)
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Mesoscopic Rough Electrical Double Layers
item.description:
Author(s): Weiqiang Tang, Jinwen Liu, Katharina Doblhoff-Dier, and Jun Huang

Fundamental understanding of electrical double layers (EDL) has been gleaned mostly on ideally planar electrodes, while realistic electrodes usually exhibit surface roughness on multiple scales. The influence of mesoscopic roughness (1–10 nm) is elusive, representing a cutting-edge challenge to theo…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 038001] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Weiqiang Tang, Jinwen Liu, Katharina Doblhoff-Dier, and Jun Huang

Fundamental understanding of electrical double layers (EDL) has been gleaned mostly on ideally planar electrodes, while realistic electrodes usually exhibit surface roughness on multiple scales. The influence of mesoscopic roughness (1–10 nm) is elusive, representing a cutting-edge challenge to theo…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 038001] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Mesoscopic Rough Electrical Double Layers
item.dc:creator: Weiqiang Tang, Jinwen Liu, Katharina Doblhoff-Dier, and Jun Huang

item.title: Fractal Spectrum in Twisted Bilayer Optical Lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Xu-Tao Wan, Chao Gao, and Zhe-Yu Shi

We investigate the full spectrum of twisted bilayer optical lattices (TBOLs) across all possible twist angles. Our calculation departs from the conventional moiré physics paradigm, which focuses on continuum theory at a fixed small twist angle. We discover that the full spectrum of a TBOL exhibits a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 033401] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xu-Tao Wan, Chao Gao, and Zhe-Yu Shi

We investigate the full spectrum of twisted bilayer optical lattices (TBOLs) across all possible twist angles. Our calculation departs from the conventional moiré physics paradigm, which focuses on continuum theory at a fixed small twist angle. We discover that the full spectrum of a TBOL exhibits a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 033401] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Fractal Spectrum in Twisted Bilayer Optical Lattice
item.dc:creator: Xu-Tao Wan, Chao Gao, and Zhe-Yu Shi

item.title: Gate-Tunable Spin Switching Effect and Bilinear Magnetoelectric Resistance in the Topological Semimetal
item.description:
Author(s): An-Qi Wang, Tong-Yang Zhao, Chuan Li, Xu-Dong Yang, Rui Zhu, Jing-Wei Dong, Alexander Brinkman, Chun-Guang Chu, and Zhi-Min Liao

Topological materials exhibit topologically protected surface states (TSS) characterized by helical spin texture and high charge-spin conversion efficiency, making them promising for high-performance spintronic devices. However, achieving gate control to switch between up and down spin-polarized sta…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036201] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): An-Qi Wang, Tong-Yang Zhao, Chuan Li, Xu-Dong Yang, Rui Zhu, Jing-Wei Dong, Alexander Brinkman, Chun-Guang Chu, and Zhi-Min Liao

Topological materials exhibit topologically protected surface states (TSS) characterized by helical spin texture and high charge-spin conversion efficiency, making them promising for high-performance spintronic devices. However, achieving gate control to switch between up and down spin-polarized sta…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036201] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Gate-Tunable Spin Switching Effect and Bilinear Magnetoelectric Resistance in the Topological Semimetal
item.dc:creator: An-Qi Wang, Tong-Yang Zhao, Chuan Li, Xu-Dong Yang, Rui Zhu, Jing-Wei Dong, Alexander Brinkman, Chun-Guang Chu, and Zhi-Min Liao
